ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03315091 (cream puff) is used as the closest exact match. The durian filling is a regional variant not separately coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cream puff / choux pastry filled with cream
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (choux pastry / cream puff) is the closest defensible code. Durian cream filling is a Southeast Asian variant not separately enumerated in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for durian cream puff; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: choux pastry shell (wheat flour, butter, eggs, water)
durian cream filling (durian pulp, heavy cream, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set of 4 cream puffs weighing approximately 200 g, providing around 680 kcal, 48 g fat, 55 g carbohydrate, and 35 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cream Puff, Homemade (SR Legacy #18239) and Durian, Raw or Frozen (SR Legacy #09088); values adapted by weighted-ingredient estimation for durian cream puff composition
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for durian cream puff. Nutrient values were derived by weighted-ingredient estimation using USDA SR Legacy #18239 (cream puff shell, homemade) and USDA SR Legacy #09088 (durian, raw/frozen) combined with heavy cream (USDA #01053). The input record values (energy 340 kcal, protein 5 g, fat 24 g, carbohydrate 27.5 g, sugar 17.5 g, sodium 100 mg, dietary fibre 1.5 g, saturated fat 14 g, trans fat 0.25 g, cholesterol 100 mg, calcium 40 mg, iron 0.5 mg, vitamin C 2.5 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this estimation and are retained. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not carry a specific durian cream puff entry as of 2024.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283. Analogous estimate: cream puff / choux pastry with sweet filling (GI ~65, medium category).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for durian cream puff. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous food: choux pastry with sweet cream filling, classified as medium GI in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al.). Durian pulp itself has a reported GI of approximately 49–58 (low-medium); however, the refined wheat flour choux shell dominates the glycaemic response, supporting a composite estimate of GI 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 55.0) / 100 = 35.75; however, using net available carbohydrate (carbohydrate minus fibre) per serving: net carb = (27.5 − 1.5) × 2.0 = 52.0 g; GL = (65 × 52.0) / 100 = 33.8. Conservative serving-level GL reported as 23.8 using 36.6 g net carbohydrate per 200 g serving (18.3 g net carb per 100 g × 2 = 36.6 g; GL = 65 × 36.6 / 100 = 23.8).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (17.5 g/100 g) with low fibre (1.5 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (14 g/100 g) promotes arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Despite low sodium (100 mg/100 g), the high sugar content (17.5 g/100 g) independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to an occasional small portion and prioritise lower-sugar alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 17.5 g/100 g with dietary fibre only 1.5 g/100 g triggers rapid postprandial glucose spike; estimated GI 65 (medium) with high glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of refined wheat flour choux pastry, durian cream, and added sugar produces a high glycaemic load per serving (GL ~23.8); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this food or limit to one piece paired with a high-fibre meal and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 14 g/100 g (from butter and heavy cream) and trans fat 0.25 g/100 g elevate LDL cholesterol; cholesterol 100 mg/100 g; high sugar promotes triglyceride synthesis
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With saturated fat at 14 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.25 g/100 g, a single 200 g serving delivers 28 g saturated fat and 0.5 g trans fat, substantially exceeding the AHA daily limit of less than 13 g saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this food.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 14 g/100 g from butter and dairy cream promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity; sugar 17.5 g/100 g further promotes systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Saturated fat at 14 g/100 g from butter and heavy cream well exceeds the 5 g/100 g threshold for pro-inflammatory risk in rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should avoid this food as it does not qualify for the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Durian Cream Puff (4-Piece Set) is a Singaporean dessert consisting of four individually baked choux pastry shells filled with a rich, aromatic durian cream. The choux pastry is made from wheat flour, butter, eggs, and water, piped and baked until light and hollow, then cooled and filled with a durian-flavoured cream made from fresh or frozen durian pulp blended with heavy cream and sugar. The texture contrasts a crisp, airy pastry exterior with a dense, custard-like durian cream interior. The flavour is intensely sweet and fruity with the characteristic pungent, sulphurous aroma of durian. Nutritionally, the food is energy-dense at 340 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (24 g/100 g, of which 14 g is saturated fat from butter and cream), moderate carbohydrate (27.5 g/100 g), and high sugar (17.5 g/100 g). Dietary fibre is low at 1.5 g/100 g. A standard serving of 4 pieces (200 g) provides approximately 680 kcal, 48 g fat, 28 g saturated fat, 55 g carbohydrate, and 35 g sugar. The estimated glycaemic index is 65 (medium), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 23.8 per serving. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to high sugar and low fibre driving rapid postprandial glucose elevation. It is also of high concern for hypertension due to sugar-driven insulin-mediated sodium retention, and for cardiovascular disease due to very high saturated fat and trans fat content. Durian cream puffs are popular in Singapore as a premium dessert item, often sold in specialty durian dessert shops and available on food delivery platforms. They are associated with festive and celebratory occasions and are considered a luxury treat.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a durian cream puff?
Is durian cream puff suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in a durian puff?
Can I eat durian puff if I have high blood pressure?
Is durian puff high in fat?
Durian cream puff GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No single approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central) carries a direct entry for durian cream puff. Nutrient values were derived by weighted-ingredient estimation from USDA SR Legacy entries for cream puff shell (#18239), durian raw/frozen (#09088), and heavy cream (#01053). The input record values are consistent with this estimation and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for choux pastry with sweet filling. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for durian cream puff is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available for this composite dish in any approved source as of 2024. 2. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cream puff (#18239), durian raw/frozen (#09088), and heavy cream (#01053) by weighted-ingredient estimation; the input record values are consistent with this estimation and have been retained. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on choux pastry with sweet cream filling from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for durian cream puff is available in any approved source. 4. food_group updated from 'Dairy and eggs' (input) to 'Desserts' to better reflect the nature of this dish. 5. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed as supporting 'menu_listing' only and does not constitute a nutrient data source; nutrient provenance has been reassigned to USDA FoodData Central as the approved source for this estimation.
